cvrp_construct.algorithm: A modified savings-based heuristic that at each step ranks feasible customers by a weighted score combining the classic Clarke-Wright savings relative to the depot and a penalty for leaving isolated clusters, then picks the highest-ranked customer unless selecting it would create an unreachable high-demand island within the route’s spatial span, in which case it triggers an early depot return.

cvrp_construct.algorithm: At each step, rank feasible customers by a regret measure that captures the opportunity loss of not visiting them now, computed as the difference between their second-best feasible insertion position’s extra cost and the cheapest immediate insertion cost, while biasing toward high-demand nodes when capacity slack is large, and return to depot when the smallest regret exceeds a dynamic threshold tied to the current route’s average regret per unit remaining capacity.

cvrp_construct.algorithm: Modified farthest-then-regret with dynamic threshold: seed routes with farthest feasible customer from depot, then switch to a hybrid regret score weighted by both proximity to current node and potential future detour penalty, using a tunable parameter to balance exploration and greediness.

cvrp_construct.algorithm: A modified far-first strategy: always start a route with the farthest feasible customer from the depot; during the route, evaluate all feasible customers by a weighted sum of (negative normalized distance from current node) and (normalized distance from depot), choosing the highest score to balance proximity and future access savings; fall back to the nearest feasible customer if all scores are equal, and return depot only when no feasible customers remain.

cvrp_construct.algorithm: A hybrid farthest-first/regret-insertion constructive heuristic that seeds routes by selecting the most distant feasible customer from the depot when near the depot, then switches to highest-regret selection among feasible customers based on current position, falling back to nearest feasible if ties occur.

cvrp_construct.algorithm: Adaptive farthest-first with regret fallback: when at the depot start a route by selecting the farthest feasible customer; when on-route, choose among feasible customers using a weighted criterion favoring high regret but penalized by distance from the current node, switching to nearest-neighbor if no high-regret candidate exists; return to depot only when no feasible customer remains.
